notes: Three discriminating evals are defined; no live benchmark results are claimed.

evals.prompt: This is an application repo with direct child folders under .agents/skills, and I only want to inspect the catalog without changing files. Which mode and command should I use, and what does it validate?
expectations: Selects project catalog mode for direct child skill folders.
States that --check is non-writing and validates fatal metadata errors and README markers.
Distinguishes a catalog check from proving that live skills or marketplace listings are safe or current.

evals.prompt: I need to refresh the repository skill inventory after adding one skill. Give me the safe preview and write commands, and tell me which files should change.
expectations: Provides the --dry-run command before the write command.
Provides the normal catalog command using --skills-dir .agents/skills.
Identifies .agents/skills/README.md between catalog markers as the generated catalog output.
Identifies .agents/skills/.catalog-meta.json as generated machine-readable metadata and instructs the agent to inspect the diff.

evals.prompt: Someone proposes running the cataloger's --full mode in this LifeTrkr application repo. Explain whether that is appropriate, what --full scans, and what safer alternative applies here.
expectations: Recommends normal project catalog mode for this application repo instead of casually using --full.
Explains that --full scans root-level family/skill folders and excludes .agents/.
States that --full can write root README.md, FAMILY.md files, and root catalog metadata.
Calls out --no-absorb-readme or --dry-run when a distribution index is intentionally required.
